what_it_does: Stores a proposed operator-memory rule stating that the AI should supply per-domain depth and surface options for Tim to decide, rather than asking Tim for detailed specs.
observations: Module docstring labels the file as MIGRATED (B5 batch 3) and awaiting Tim's confirmation.
The file contains a single top-level MEMORY dictionary with no executable code.
MEMORY has keys: id, rule, why, evidence, scope, status.
The rule defines a collaboration boundary: Tim designs across domains; the AI brings specialist depth.
Evidence is a single quoted note attributed to a 'standing harness note, 2026-06-02'.
Status is 'proposed', not active or confirmed.
standouts: The memory encodes a role-inversion norm: the AI, not Tim, is expected to produce domain depth.
It explicitly forbids demanding specs from Tim and requires surfacing options.
It is in a provisional 'proposed' migration state pending confirmation.
conventions: Follows an operator-memory schema with id/rule/why/evidence/scope/status.
Uses a module-level dictionary literal and a docstring migration annotation.
Cites a source note for provenance.
concerns: Status is 'proposed' and unconfirmed, so the rule is not yet authoritative.
Contains no code or enforcement mechanism; it is purely declarative.
Evidence relies on a single self-referential source whose content is not independently verifiable from this file.
notes: A proposed collaboration-policy memory that tells the AI to bring domain options instead of asking Tim for specs, currently awaiting confirmation.
questions: What does 'B5 batch 3' refer to and what migration process is it part of?
What does Tim's confirmation change about the memory's status or effect?
How is this memory loaded, validated, or enforced by the system?
What is the 'standing harness note' and where is it stored?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records a collaboration principle for Tim-AI decision making, not executable logic.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to institutionalize a collaboration contract: Tim owns vision and decisions across domains, while the AI owns generating deep, per-domain options so Tim can choose without being treated as a spec source.
novelty: It encodes an inverse-responsibility collaboration norm as structured, cited operator memory with a migration/confirmation lifecycle.
contribution: A declarative, cited policy memory that defines decision-role boundaries and a 'surface options, don't demand specs' workflow for Tim-AI collaboration; fusion would keep it as a normative guardrail.
summary_for_embedding: A proposed operator-memory entry that instructs the AI to provide per-domain depth and present options for Tim to decide, rather than requesting specifications from him.
intention_for_embedding: To encode a collaboration rule that reserves cross-domain decision ownership for Tim while assigning specialist option-generation to the AI.
